explore.heading: Explore Time Zones
explore.description: Pick any point on the globe to see the local time, UTC offset, and daylight saving status. Use Explore to understand where time changes across borders.
articleParagraphs: The interactive 3D globe turns time zones into something you can see rather than calculate. Click any location and Timezio reads its exact latitude and longitude, looks up the matching IANA time zone, and shows the current local time, the UTC offset, and whether daylight saving time is currently in effect. Because the data comes from the official IANA time zone database, it covers the awkward cases too — half-hour offsets like India (UTC+5:30), 45-minute offsets like Nepal, and regions that change their clocks on different dates.

Explore is the quickest way to answer “what time is it there right now?” without typing a city name. Spin the globe to a continent, click a coastline, and read the time instantly. When you want to line up several places at once, switch to Compare; when you need to find a slot that works for a team, jump to the Planner. Every view stays in sync, so a city you click here is ready to compare or schedule with one tap.

Time zones are political as much as geographic — borders, exceptions, and seasonal rules all shift the clock. Seeing them on a globe makes those boundaries obvious, which is why Explore is a good starting point before you convert a specific time or book an international call.

compare.heading: Compare Time Zones
compare.description: Compare multiple time zones side-by-side with DST-aware offsets. Add cities, choose a base location, and verify the time difference before you schedule.
ctas: Best meeting times by city pair
articleParagraphs: Add up to six cities and Timezio lines up their current local times in a single view. Pick one as the “base” and every other city shows its difference relative to it, so you can answer questions like “when it’s 9:00 AM in Berlin, what time is it in New York and Singapore?” at a glance. The offsets are calculated from official IANA data and update in real time, including the one-hour shifts that happen when a region enters or leaves daylight saving time.

Comparing is most useful when the difference isn’t obvious. Many country pairs sit a fractional number of hours apart, and several change their clocks on different weekends, so the gap between two cities can be nine hours one month and ten the next. Lining the cities up side by side catches those edge cases before they turn into a missed call. For a date in the future, change the date and the comparison recalculates with the correct DST rules for that day.
When you’ve confirmed the difference, copy the share link to send the exact comparison to your team, or move the same set of cities into the Planner to find a meeting slot that works for everyone.

planner.heading: Meeting Planner
planner.description: Plan meetings across multiple time zones. Add participants, set working hours, and find overlap windows automatically — fully DST-aware.
ctas: Best meeting times by city pair
planner.articleH2: Find a time that works for everyone
articleParagraphs: Add each participant by city, set their working hours, and the planner overlays every schedule to surface the windows when everyone is available. Instead of mentally juggling offsets, you get a ranked list of slots that fall inside each person’s working day, with early-morning and late-night times de-prioritized so nobody has to take a 6 a.m. call. Every calculation is daylight-saving aware, so a slot that works this week still works after a region changes its clocks.

The planner is built for distributed teams, client calls across continents, and catching up with friends or family abroad. Set a meeting duration, choose a date, and adjust each participant’s hours if they work an unusual shift. You can also widen the search when there is no clean overlap — a common situation for pairs that are ten or more hours apart, where someone has to take an edge slot. For Google Calendar invites and Meet links, connect your account in the planner and confirm a slot.
Already know the two cities? The pre-built best-meeting-time pages show the overlap for popular pairs without any setup.

saved.heading: Saved
saved.description: Save your frequently used locations so you can return instantly to their current time, offset, and daylight saving status.
ctas: Plan a meeting across time zones

convertHub.title: Popular Time Zone Converters
convertHub.intro1: A time zone converter answers a deceptively tricky question: when it’s a given time in one place, what time is it somewhere else? The catch is that the gap between two zones isn’t fixed. Daylight saving time shifts it by an hour, the two regions often change their clocks on different weekends, and some zones sit a fractional number of hours apart. Each converter below uses official IANA data, so the result is correct on any date — including the weeks around a clock change when manual math usually goes wrong.
convertHub.intro2: Every converter page includes a full hour-by-hour table for the current day, the live offset between the two zones, and notes on which side observes daylight saving time. Need to check a city rather than an abbreviation? Browse the <world>world clock</world>, or jump to <compare>Compare</compare> to line up more than two places at once.

timeHub.title: Current Time in Major Cities Worldwide
timeHub.intro1: Pick a city to see its current local time, the exact UTC offset, the IANA time zone it uses, and whether daylight saving time is in effect right now. Every page updates in real time and is calculated from the official IANA time zone database, so the awkward cases — half-hour offsets, regions that change clocks on different dates, and places that don’t observe DST at all — are handled correctly.
timeHub.intro2: These world-clock pages are the quickest way to check “what time is it there?” before you call, travel, or schedule. To convert a specific time between two places use the <convert>converters</convert>, to line up several cities use <compare>Compare</compare>, and to find a slot that works for a group use the <planner>Meeting Planner</planner>.

meetingHub.title: Best Meeting Times by City Pair
meetingHub.intro1: Scheduling across continents usually means someone takes a call at a bad hour. These pages do the hard part for you: for each popular city pair, Timezio overlays both teams’ standard 9-to-5 day and lists the hours when everyone is awake and working, plus a recommended slot in the middle of that window. Because the math uses official IANA data, the overlap stays correct even when one city changes its clocks and the other doesn’t.
meetingHub.intro2: Pick a pair below for an instant answer, or open the <planner>Meeting Planner</planner> to add your own cities, adjust each participant’s working hours, and generate calendar invites. To double-check the raw time difference first, use <compare>Compare</compare>.

dstHub.title: Daylight Saving Time by Country
dstHub.intro1: Daylight saving time is one of the biggest sources of scheduling mistakes: clocks move an hour, but not everywhere and not on the same day. These pages tell you, for each region, whether DST is currently in effect, what the present UTC offset is, and the exact dates of the next spring-forward and fall-back transitions — all computed from the official IANA time zone database rather than hard-coded rules that go stale.
dstHub.intro2: Knowing when a region changes its clocks helps you protect recurring meetings, travel plans, and deadlines that span borders. Choose a region below, then use <compare>Compare</compare> or the <planner>Meeting Planner</planner> to see how an upcoming change shifts the time difference with your own location.

time.introMain: The current local time in {city} is {time} on {date}. {city} runs on the {tz} time zone, which is presently {offsetAbbr}.
time.dstSentence: {observesDst, select, yes {{city} observes daylight saving time, so its UTC offset shifts by one hour between standard and summer time.} other {{city} does not observe daylight saving time, so its UTC offset ({offset}) stays the same all year.}}
time.transitionLabel: {type, select, begins {Clocks spring forward — DST begins} other {Clocks fall back — DST ends}}
time.nextChange: Next clock change: {date} — {label}. The UTC offset moves from {fromOffset} to {toOffset}. Plan calls and travel around this date to avoid being an hour off.
time.crosslinks: Use the interactive globe above to compare {city} with other cities, or open the <compare>Compare</compare> tab to line up several time zones side by side. Planning a call? The <planner>Meeting Planner</planner> finds overlapping working hours automatically, and the <dst>DST checker</dst> tracks upcoming clock changes.
time.wpDesc: Live local time in {city} ({tz}). Current UTC offset {offsetAbbr}, with daylight saving status and the next clock change.
time.faq1q: What time is it in {city} right now?
time.faq1a: As of {date}, the local time in {city} is around {time} ({abbr}). The clock at the top of this page ticks in real time, and the offset shown is {offset}.
time.faq2a: {city} uses the {tz} IANA time zone{hasAbbr, select, yes {, currently abbreviated {abbr}} other {}}. Its current offset from Coordinated Universal Time is {offset}.
time.faq3a: {observesDst, select, yes {Yes. {city} observes daylight saving time, so its UTC offset shifts by one hour between standard and summer time.{hasNext, select, yes { The next change is on {nextDate}, when the clocks {nextDir, select, forward {move forward} other {move back}} and the offset becomes {nextOffset}.} other {}}} other {{city} does not observe daylight saving time, so its UTC offset ({offset}) stays the same all year.}}
time.faq4q: What is the UTC offset for {city}?
time.faq4a: {city} is currently at {offset}. {observesDst, select, yes {This offset changes by one hour during the daylight saving period.} other {This offset is fixed throughout the year.}}

convert.diffSummary: {dir, select, same {{to} is currently the same time as {from}.} ahead {{to} is currently {h}h{m, plural, =0 {} other { #m}} ahead of {from}.} other {{to} is currently {h}h{m, plural, =0 {} other { #m}} behind {from}.}}
convert.introBody: {from} is {fromFull}; {to} is {toFull}. The table below shows the conversion for every part of the day, with daylight saving time handled automatically.
convert.crosslinks: Need a specific date or a different pair? Use the interactive globe and the <compare>Compare</compare> tab to preview any moment, including future dates that cross a daylight saving boundary. To schedule a call between these zones, try the <planner>Meeting Planner</planner>.
convert.faq1q: What is the time difference between {from} and {to}?
convert.faq1a: Right now {to} ({toFull}) is {dir, select, same {the same time as} ahead {ahead of} other {behind}} {from} ({fromFull}). The difference is {absH, plural, =0 {zero hours} one {# hour} other {# hours}} and can shift by an hour when either zone enters or leaves daylight saving time.
convert.faq2a: Add the {from} time to the offset difference. For example, {exampleFrom} {from} is {exampleTo} {to}. The full hour-by-hour table on this page lists each conversion for the current date.
convert.faq3q: Does {from} or {to} use daylight saving time?
convert.faq3a: {from} is currently {fromDst, select, yes {in a region that observes DST} other {on a fixed offset (no DST)}} ({fromOffset}), and {to} is {toDst, select, yes {in a region that observes DST} other {on a fixed offset (no DST)}} ({toOffset}). The converter uses official IANA data and adjusts automatically.
convert.faq4a: Yes — conversions use the IANA time zone database, the same source operating systems use, and account for current daylight saving rules to the minute.

meeting.overlapSentence: {hasOverlap, select, yes {Standard 9-to-5 working hours in {a} and {b} overlap from {start} to {end} {a} time. A good middle slot is around {bestA} in {a} ({bestB} in {b}).} other {Standard 9-to-5 working hours in {a} and {b} do not overlap, so one side will need an early or late slot. Aim for the start of one city's day and the end of the other's.}}
meeting.introTail: All times below are for today and adjust automatically for daylight saving time.
meeting.noOverlapBody: No 9-to-5 overlap today. Open the Planner above to widen working hours or pick an edge slot that works for both teams.
meeting.crosslinks: The Planner tab above already has {a} and {b} loaded. Adjust each participant's hours, set a duration, and Timezio ranks the best slots. To double-check the raw offset, use the <compare>Compare</compare> tab.
meeting.wpDesc: Find the best meeting time between {a} and {b}. {overlap}
meeting.faq1q: What is the best time to schedule a meeting between {a} and {b}?
meeting.faq1a: {hasOverlap, select, yes {The cleanest option is around {bestA} in {a}, which is {bestB} in {b} — inside both teams' working day. The overlap table on this page lists every shared hour for today.} other {There is no clean overlap during standard hours, so pick the least disruptive edge: early morning for one city or early evening for the other. The table on this page shows each hour side by side.}}
meeting.faq2q: What is the time difference between {a} and {b}?
meeting.faq2a: {a} is currently {offsetA} and {b} is {offsetB}. The difference can move by an hour around daylight saving transitions, which this tool accounts for automatically.
meeting.faq3q: How do I plan a recurring call across {a} and {b}?
meeting.faq3a: Use the Planner tab above — both cities are pre-loaded. Set each participant's working hours and Timezio highlights overlapping slots and warns about early-morning or late-night times.

dst.intro: {observesDst, select, yes {{region} observes daylight saving time. Clocks are currently {isDst, select, yes {on summer time (DST in effect)} other {on standard time}}, at {offset}. The reference zone for {region} is {tz} ({city}).} other {{region} does not currently observe daylight saving time. The offset stays at {offset} all year. The reference zone is {tz} ({city}).}}
dst.crosslinks: Daylight saving changes shift {region}'s offset by an hour, which can quietly break recurring meetings. Check the live difference against your own zone with the <compare>Compare</compare> tab, or schedule around the change using the <planner>Meeting Planner</planner>.
dst.faq1a: {observesDst, select, yes {Yes. {region} changes its clocks twice a year. It is currently {isDst, select, yes {on daylight saving (summer) time} other {on standard time}} at {offset}.} other {No. {region} keeps a fixed offset of {offset} throughout the year and does not change its clocks.}}
dst.faq2q: When do the clocks change in {region}?
dst.faq2a: {observesDst, select, yes {{hasBegins, select, yes {Clocks next spring forward on {beginsDate} (offset {beginsFrom} → {beginsTo}). } other {}}{hasEnds, select, yes {Clocks next fall back on {endsDate} (offset {endsFrom} → {endsTo}).} other {}}} other {{region} does not change its clocks, so there is no spring-forward or fall-back date.}}
dst.faq3q: What is the current UTC offset for {region}?
dst.faq3a: {region} is currently at {offset}{hasAbbr, select, yes { ({abbr})} other {}}. {observesDst, select, yes {This shifts by one hour when daylight saving begins or ends.} other {This offset is fixed.}}

intro.text: This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and protect information when you visit and use this website (the "Website").
We are committed to protecting your privacy and processing personal data in accordance with applicable data protection laws, including the General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR).

blocks.text: 1.1 Information You Do Not Provide Directly
This Website does not require user registration and does not ask users to provide personal information to use its core features.
1.2 Automatically Collected Information
When you visit the Website, limited information may be collected automatically, including:

items: IP address (anonymized/truncated where supported by the tool)
Approximate geographic location (city or country level)

blocks.text: This information is used only for analytics, security, and service improvement purposes.

Core tools (time conversion, planner) work without registration. If you choose to save/share meeting data, connect a calendar, or sign in with Google, we may store the meeting/session details you submit (e.g., participant names or labels, time slots, titles) and your Google account email to enable the feature. You can request deletion of this stored data at any time (see Contact).
The Website may use cookies or similar technologies to:

items: Understand how users interact with the Website

blocks.text: Cookies used on this Website do not directly identify you as an individual.
Where required by applicable law, cookies that are not strictly necessary are used only after obtaining user consent through a cookie banner or consent mechanism.
You can manage or withdraw your consent at any time through your browser settings or the cookie settings provided on the Website.
3.1 Analytics

We may use analytics tools (e.g., Google Analytics via Google Tag Manager or privacy-focused alternatives) to understand how visitors use the Website. These tools collect usage data (pages viewed, device/browser info, approximate location) and can be configured to anonymize IP where supported. Non-essential analytics only load after consent where required.
3.2 Advertising

The Website may display advertisements from third-party partners such as Google AdSense (served via Google Tag Manager). These partners may use cookies or similar technologies to display relevant ads and may process data (e.g., cookie IDs, coarse location, device info). Advertising cookies are loaded only after consent where required.

Advertising partners process information under their own privacy policies and may transfer data outside your region under appropriate safeguards (e.g., Standard Contractual Clauses). You can withdraw or change your cookie and advertising consent at any time using the **“Cookie settings”** link in the website footer, or through your browser settings.
You can also control or opt out of personalized advertising directly with the providers and industry programs:

items: How Google uses data from partner sites — [policies.google.com/technologies/partner-sites](https://policies.google.com/technologies/partner-sites)

blocks.text: Collected information may be used to:
**We do not sell personal data.**
Under the GDPR, personal data is processed on the following legal bases:

items: **Legitimate interests** — to operate, secure, and improve the Website
**Legal obligation** — where processing is required by law

blocks.text: We retain collected data only for as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes described in this Privacy Policy, unless a longer retention period is required or permitted by law.
We may share limited data with trusted third-party service providers (such as analytics or advertising providers) strictly for the purposes described in this Privacy Policy.
All such providers are required to process data in compliance with applicable data protection laws.

Some providers (e.g., Google) may process data outside the European Economic Area (EEA). When that occurs, we rely on appropriate safeguards such as Standard Contractual Clauses or equivalent mechanisms.
If you are located in the European Union, you have the right to:

items: Lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority

blocks.text: You may exercise these rights by contacting us using the details provided below.

sections.heading: 9. US State Privacy Rights (CCPA / CPRA & similar)

blocks.text: If you are a resident of California or another US state with comprehensive privacy laws, you may have the right to know what personal information is collected, to request access or deletion, to correct inaccurate information, and to opt out of the “sale” or “sharing” of personal information and of targeted advertising.

We do not sell personal information for money. However, the use of advertising cookies may be considered “sharing” or a “sale” under some state laws. You can opt out of personalized advertising using the **“Cookie settings”** link in the footer and the industry opt-out tools listed in Section 3.2. Where supported, we honor the Global Privacy Control (GPC) browser signal as a valid opt-out request.
To exercise any of these rights, contact us using the details in Section 14. We will not discriminate against you for exercising your privacy rights.
This Website is not intended for children under the age of 16. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children.

We take reasonable technical and organizational measures to protect information against unauthorized access, loss, misuse, or alteration. However, no method of transmission or storage is completely secure.
The Website may contain links to third-party websites. We are not responsible for the content or privacy practices of those websites.
We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Any changes will be posted on this page with an updated "Last updated" date.
We encourage users to review this page periodically. If changes materially affect how we process your data, we will provide a clear notice (e.g., on this page or via the Website interface).
If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or how data is processed on this Website, you may contact us at:

intro.text: These Terms of Service ("Terms") govern your access to and use of this website (the "Website"). By accessing or using the Website, you agree to be bound by these Terms. If you do not agree, please discontinue use of the Website.

blocks.text: The Website provides tools and information related to time zones, time differences, UTC conversions, and meeting planning for general informational purposes only.
The Website does not provide legal, financial, professional, or time-critical advisory services.
You agree to use the Website only for lawful purposes and in a manner that does not:

items: Interfere with or disrupt the operation of the Website
Attempt to gain unauthorized access to any part of the Website

blocks.text: We reserve the right to restrict or terminate access to the Website at any time if misuse is detected.

While we make reasonable efforts to ensure that the information and calculations provided on the Website are accurate and based on recognized public time zone data, we do not guarantee that the content is complete, current, or error-free.
Time zone rules and daylight saving time practices may change due to governmental or regulatory decisions. You acknowledge that any reliance on the information provided is at your own risk.
The Website and all content and tools are provided on an "as is" and "as available" basis.

To the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, we disclaim all warranties, whether express or implied, including but not limited to implied warranties of accuracy, reliability, fitness for a particular purpose, and non-infringement.

To the extent permitted by applicable European Union law, we shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, consequential, or special damages arising out of or in connection with your use of, or inability to use, the Website or reliance on its content.
This includes, without limitation, loss of data, loss of business, missed meetings, or scheduling errors.
All content on the Website, including text, design, layout, logos, and software, is the property of the Website owner or its licensors and is protected by applicable intellectual property laws.

You may use the Website for personal and non-commercial purposes. You may not copy, reproduce, distribute, modify, or exploit any part of the Website without prior written permission, except where permitted by law.
The Website may display advertisements or include links to third-party websites or services.

We do not control and are not responsible for the content, availability, or practices of third-party websites. Your interaction with third-party services is governed by their respective terms and privacy policies.
Your use of the Website is also governed by our [Privacy Policy](/privacy), which explains how personal data is processed in accordance with applicable data protection laws, including the GDPR.

sections.heading: 9. Changes to the Website or Terms

blocks.text: We may update, modify, suspend, or discontinue any part of the Website at any time without prior notice.
We may also update these Terms from time to time. Any changes will be effective once posted on this page, with the "Last updated" date revised accordingly.
Continued use of the Website after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated Terms.
These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the European Union and, where applicable, the laws of the country in which the Website operator is established.
Nothing in these Terms limits your statutory rights as a consumer under applicable EU law.
If any provision of these Terms is found to be unlawful, invalid, or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.
If you have any questions regarding these Terms of Service, you may contact us at:

intro.text: This website was created to make working across time zones simple, accurate, and stress-free. Whether you're planning a meeting with colleagues abroad, checking the current time in another city, converting UTC, or traveling across countries, our tools help you instantly understand time differences without confusion.

blocks.text: Timezio is built and operated by **AffSquad**, an independent studio that makes free, privacy-respecting web utilities. We're a small team that relies on these tools ourselves to coordinate across time zones, and we keep Timezio free by displaying ads. Questions, corrections, or feedback are always welcome at [hello@timezio.com](mailto:hello@timezio.com) — see the [Contact](/contact) page for more ways to reach us.
Accuracy
All calculations are based on official IANA time zone data and are fully daylight-saving-time (DST) aware.
Simplicity
Clean, fast tools that work instantly without unnecessary steps.
Reliability
Built for daily use by professionals, travelers, remote teams, and digital nomads.
Our calculators automatically account for:

items: Date changes when crossing time zones
Regions with non-standard offsets (such as 30- or 45-minute differences)

blocks.text: This website is designed to be useful worldwide and is updated regularly to reflect official time zone rules.

items: Anyone who needs to convert or compare time zones accurately

blocks.text: **No accounts. No complexity. Just clear answers.**
1. Explore — Interactive Globe
Click anywhere on the 3D globe to instantly see the local time for that location.
**How it works:** Click a location on the globe or search for a city. View the current time, UTC offset, and DST status. Perfect for quick checks and exploring time zones visually.
2. Compare — Time Zone Comparison
Compare time differences between multiple cities simultaneously.

**How it works:** Add up to 6 cities to compare. Select a base city to see time differences relative to it. Instantly see current local times, offsets, and visual comparisons. This is ideal for answering questions like "When it's 9:00 AM in Berlin, what time is it in New York?"
3. Planner — Meeting Planner
Find the best meeting time for people in different locations.

**How it works:** Add participants by city, select a meeting date, and optionally adjust working hours for each participant. View overlapping time windows and share the meeting details. This tool automatically avoids early-morning or late-night hours when possible.
4. DST — Daylight Saving Time
Track daylight saving time changes and transitions throughout the year.
**How it works:** View DST status for any location, see upcoming transitions, and understand when clocks change. Check how DST affects time differences between regions.
5. Saved — Favorite Locations
Save your frequently used locations for quick access.
**How it works:** Save locations you check often. Access them instantly without searching. Your saved locations are stored locally in your browser.
What time zone data does this website use?
We use official IANA time zone definitions, which are the global standard for accurate time calculations.
Does the website account for daylight saving time?
Yes. All calculations automatically adjust for daylight saving time based on the selected date and location.
Can I check future or past dates?
Yes. You can select any date and time to see how time differences change throughout the year.
Why does the time difference change during the year?
Time differences change because some regions observe daylight saving time while others do not, or they change clocks on different dates.
Are cities with the same name supported?
Yes. Cities are identified by both name and country to avoid confusion (for example, multiple cities named "Springfield").
Is this website free to use?
Yes. All tools on this website are completely free to use and require no registration.
Can I share or bookmark results?
Yes. Tool pages generate clean, shareable URLs that you can bookmark or send to others using the "Copy link" button.
How accurate are the results?
Results are accurate to the minute and based on official time zone rules, but should not be used for legal or critical decisions without verification.

intro.text: We value your feedback and are happy to hear from you.
If you have questions about how our tools work, would like to report an issue, or want to share suggestions for improvement, please feel free to get in touch.
Timezio is operated by **AffSquad**, an independent studio that builds free web utilities.

blocks.text: You can contact us by email:
We aim to respond to messages within a reasonable time. Please note that response times may vary depending on the volume of inquiries.

sections.heading: What You Can Contact Us About

blocks.text: You may contact us regarding:

items: Questions about time zone calculations or tool functionality
Technical issues or bugs on the website

blocks.text: **Please note:** We are unable to provide personalized scheduling advice, legal guidance, or time-critical confirmations.
If you contact us by email, we will process any personal data you provide (such as your email address and message content) solely for the purpose of responding to your inquiry.
Your information will be handled in accordance with applicable data protection laws, including the General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR).
For more details, please refer to our [Privacy Policy](/privacy).

The information provided through this website and via email responses is for general informational purposes only and should not be relied upon for legal, financial, aviation, or other time-critical decisions.

converter.heading: Time Zone Converter
converter.description: Explore and convert time zones on an interactive globe, check local times, and find overlaps for meetings. Fully DST-aware and powered by official IANA data.

exploreTab.searchCitySubtitle: Find any city around the world
exploreTab.popularCitiesSubtitle: Click on a city to view its time

compareTab.subtitle: Add 2–6 locations and compare offsets/time. (MVP: offsets + diff)
compareTab.noItems: No compare items. Add a city or “Add selected”.

dstTab.title: DST
dstTab.bestEffort: Best-effort: Luxon doesn’t expose IANA transition tables, so we scan forward until offset changes.
dstTab.transitionNote: Note: Transition timestamp is approximate (minute-level). For exact changes you’d need a tzdb transition table.
dstTab.infrequentChange: Some timezones don’t change offset often (or at all).

savedTab.title: Saved
savedTab.subtitle: Stored in localStorage (ids only). Use share link to carry full payload.
savedTab.emptyState: No saved locations yet. Save from Explore.

plannerTab.intro: Add participants from different time zones, set their working hours, and find the best meeting times. Choose between automatic slot finding or manual time selection. All calculations account for daylight saving time.

plannerTab.manualTimePickDesc: You choose a specific time, and the system converts it to all participants' time zones

plannerTab.autoBullet2: Finds time windows where everyone is available
plannerTab.autoBullet3: Generates slots every 15 minutes within overlap

plannerTab.autoBullet5: Shows best options first (sorted by quality score)

plannerTab.manualBullet3: Displays the same moment in all other timezones
plannerTab.manualBullet4: Perfect for fixed meeting times or specific schedules
